title: 2 Magnificent S&P 500 Dividend Stocks Down as Much as 25% to Buy and Hold Forever
author: Todd Shriber
description: Domino's Pizza and Las Vegas Sands are out-of-favor consumer cyclical stocks trading significantly below their 52-week highs. Despite recent headwinds—including disappointing earnings for Domino's and constrained hotel supply for Las Vegas Sands—both companies demonstrate commitment to shareholders through dividend increases and share buybacks, presenting potential long-term opportunities for dividend investors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock is down 36.7% from 52-week high due to disappointing Q1 results and Berkshire Hathaway selling position. However, management is signaling confidence through a new $1 billion share repurchase program and has increased dividends for 14 consecutive years, indicating strong commitment to shareholder returns despite near-term headwinds.

Stock is down 29.6% from 52-week high amid casino sector weakness. However, the company operates highly profitable properties in Macau and Singapore with expected record visitor numbers. Management is actively repurchasing shares ($3 billion since last year) and has shown consecutive dividend increases, suggesting undervaluation and long-term recovery potential.

The sector ETF is down 1.2% year-to-date while the S&P 500 is up 8.6%, indicating significant underperformance driven by macroeconomic factors including sticky inflation and weak consumer sentiment affecting consumer discretionary stocks.

title: 3 Dividend Stocks to Buy and Hold Forever
author: Todd Shriber
description: The article recommends three dividend stocks for long-term income investors seeking to reduce volatility amid economic uncertainty. PepsiCo is praised for 54 years of consecutive dividend increases and strategic repositioning toward healthy products. McDonald's offers a 49-year dividend growth streak with 9% expected annual growth and renewed focus on value pricing. Las Vegas Sands, a casino operator, presents a speculative option with restored and growing dividends, backed by profitable operations and investment-grade credit ratings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: 54 consecutive years of dividend increases, strong balance sheet with $9.5B cash, strategic pivot to healthy and value-oriented products positioning it well with cost-conscious consumers, and capacity to continue growing payouts.

49-year dividend increase streak with expected 9% annual growth over the next decade, renewed focus on value meals addressing consumer inflation concerns, and investments in AI and in-store technology to improve unit economics.

Dividend restored in 2023 with two subsequent increases, operates world's most profitable casino (Marina Bay Sands), strong cash flow generation, investment-grade credit ratings rare in casino industry, and active share buyback program.

title: Microsoft Stock's Worst Day Since March 2020, Bitcoin Plunges 5%: What's Moving Markets Thursday?
author: Piero Cingari
description: Microsoft experienced its worst trading day since March 2020, plunging 12% and erasing $400 billion in market cap despite beating earnings expectations, as investors focused on slowing Azure cloud growth and cautious AI monetization guidance. The selloff dragged major tech indices lower, with the S&P 500 down 1% and Nasdaq 100 down 1.6%. Bitcoin fell 5% to $85,000, while travel stocks surged and Meta bucked the tech weakness with strong earnings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock plunged 12% despite beating earnings and revenue, marking worst day since March 2020. Investors concerned about slowing Azure cloud growth and cautious AI monetization guidance.
Stock jumped 8.5% after strong Q4 beat, upbeat Q1 revenue guidance, and plans for higher capital spending tied to AI and advertising growth.
Stock climbed 6% after delivering better-than-expected quarterly results and guidance.
Stock cratered 16% after disappointing results and outlook weighed on sentiment across enterprise software sector.
Stock plunged 12% despite solid headline results due to forward-looking signals pointing to slowing momentum.
Stock slid 8% as part of broader software sector weakness.
Stock fell 5% amid software sector selloff.
Stock sank 13.5% after issuing weak guidance tied to softer trends in Macau.
Stock jumped 15% after positive earnings, leading travel sector rally.
Stock advanced 14% after positive earnings, contributing to travel sector surge.
Stock soared 5% following upbeat earnings and guidance, with January gains exceeding 30%.
Stock plunged 14% after missing sales expectations, marking worst S&P 500 performance.
Stock slid 11% after BMO Capital Markets downgraded from 'Outperform' to 'Market Perform' and cut price target.

title: Why Las Vegas Sands Stock Is Falling After Q4 Double Beat
author: Chris Katje
description: Las Vegas Sands beat Q4 analyst estimates with $3.649 billion in revenue (vs. $3.328 billion estimate) and $0.85 EPS (vs. $0.76 estimate), with adjusted property EBITDA growing to $1.41 billion. However, the stock fell 8.59% in after-hours trading, driven by concerns about declining margins and net income in the Macao segment despite strong Singapore operations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite beating revenue and EPS estimates, the stock declined 8.59% in after-hours trading due to investor concerns about deteriorating margins and net income in the Macao segment, which offset strong Singapore performance. The market appears to have focused on profitability concerns rather than top-line beats.

title: Casino Hotels Global Market Forecast Report 2026-2032 | How Casino Hotels are Redefining Value Amid Rising Expectations
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global casino hotel market is projected to grow from $213.09 billion in 2026 to $285.84 billion by 2032, with a CAGR of 4.9%. Growth is driven by digital transformation, personalized guest experiences, and ecosystem-centric business models. Regional strategies must adapt to local regulations and consumer preferences, while operators focus on digital innovation and supply chain resilience.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a major competitor in the growing casino hotel market with positive industry tailwinds from digital transformation and ecosystem development strategies.
Major market player positioned to benefit from industry growth driven by digital innovation, personalized experiences, and portfolio orchestration strategies.
Key competitor well-positioned to capitalize on market expansion and digital transformation trends that are central to the forecasted 4.9% CAGR growth.
Major operator in the growing market with opportunities to leverage digital ecosystems and regional expansion strategies highlighted in the forecast.
Significant market player positioned to benefit from industry growth and the shift toward experience-led, digitally-integrated resort models.
Competitive player in the expanding market with opportunities to enhance digital transformation and guest experience offerings.
